description: Acquired the remaining 37% minority stake in Global Atlantic Financial Group for approximately $2.7B, bringing full ownership of the insurance and retirement services platform in-house and consolidating ~$160B of insurance AUM under KKR's direct control.
stated_rationale: Management described full ownership as necessary to unlock operational integration efficiencies, align incentives for product development, and simplify KKR's earnings narrative for investors by eliminating the minority drag on consolidated results.
observable_metric_impact: Consolidated ~$160B of Global Atlantic AUM; eliminated minority interest deduction from earnings; insurance segment FRE fully attributable to KKR shareholders from Q1 2024.
op_prereq_summary: Required negotiation with Global Atlantic founders on buyout terms, state insurance regulatory approvals across 20+ jurisdictions, and integration of GA's investment operations into KKR's asset management platform.

description: Achieved inclusion in the S&P 500 index in 2024, following the C-Corp conversion completed in 2018 and sustained growth in market capitalization and public float that met the index committee's eligibility thresholds.
stated_rationale: Management had long identified index inclusion as a strategic objective of the C-Corp conversion, noting that passive fund flows and index-mandated buying would structurally support KKR's trading multiple and reduce cost of equity.
observable_metric_impact: S&P 500 inclusion triggered estimated $5-8B of passive index fund buying; KKR's average daily trading volume increased materially; forward P/E multiple expanded by 2-3 turns in the months following inclusion.
op_prereq_summary: Required sustained market capitalization above S&P 500 threshold, positive GAAP earnings for four consecutive quarters, sufficient public float, and meeting the index committee's qualitative governance criteria.

description: Scaled K-Series retail-oriented semi-liquid investment products to over $35B in AUM by end 2025, spanning private equity, credit, real estate, and infrastructure strategies accessible to high-net-worth and mass-affluent investors through wealth management platforms.
stated_rationale: Management cited the wealth channel as KKR's largest growth opportunity, projecting that individual investors would allocate 5-10% of portfolios to alternatives over the next decade and that semi-liquid structures would capture the majority of incremental flows.
observable_metric_impact: K-Series AUM grew from ~$10B (2022) to $35B+ (end 2025); wealth channel represented the fastest-growing capital source; K-Series products generated management fees at rates comparable to institutional drawdown funds.
op_prereq_summary: Required development of semi-liquid fund structures with periodic tender mechanisms, distribution agreements with major wirehouses and RIA platforms, retail-grade reporting and compliance infrastructure, and dedicated wealth solutions sales team.

description: Acquired a majority stake in Arctos Sports Partners, a GP stakes and sports franchise investment platform, expanding KKR's presence in the emerging sports and media private equity vertical and gaining exposure to professional sports team ownership economics.
stated_rationale: Management described sports franchises as scarce, appreciating assets with media rights inflation tailwinds and limited supply, positioning the Arctos acquisition as a differentiated entry point into a structurally growing asset class.
observable_metric_impact: Added Arctos' portfolio of minority stakes in professional sports franchises; expanded KKR's private equity AUM; provided a platform for future sports-related capital deployment across media, venues, and technology.
op_prereq_summary: Required league-level approval from NBA, NHL, and other professional sports governing bodies for change of control; integration of Arctos' GP relationships and co-investment networks into KKR's platform.
